description: Layer-blending render settings contributed by the Timing panel — fade in / fade out, in/out transition type and adjustments, effect-layer mix method, canvas blending, layer morph, and frame suppress/freeze. Despite the name, this panel has almost nothing to do with timing tracks; it's the 'how this effect blends with the layer above/below' panel. Serialized with the T_ prefix; CopySettingsMap at src-core/render/Effect.cpp:690 strips the prefix before passing to PixelBuffer::SetLayerSettings (src-core/render/PixelBuffer.cpp:2077).

properties.label: Reset panel when changing effects
properties.description: UI-only preference — when true, the panel clears back to defaults each time the selected effect changes. NOT consumed by the render engine. Included here because it is serialized under the T_ prefix alongside the render settings.

properties.label: Suppress Effect Until Frame
properties.description: Frame number before which the effect renders as black / invisible. Used to hold the layer off until a specific frame inside the effect's timespan.

properties.label: Freeze Effect At Frame
properties.description: Frame number at which the effect stops advancing. The last-rendered frame is held for the remainder of the effect's timespan. Default 999999 means 'never freeze'.

properties.label: Morph
properties.description: When two effects overlap on the same layer, gradually cross-fade Effect 1 to Effect 2 across the overlap instead of hard-switching.

properties.label: Effect Layer Mix
properties.description: Mixing threshold / ratio used by the selected LayerMethod. Interpretation depends on the method — for mask/unmask it's the cutoff, for blend methods it's the blend ratio. Stored as 0-100 and divided by 100 at render time.

properties.label: Layer Method
properties.description: How this layer blends with the one underneath. Normal is 'replace'; mask/unmask use EffectLayerMix as a cutoff; Additive/Subtractive/Min/Max are per-channel math operations.

properties.label: Canvas
properties.description: Enables canvas blending mode where this layer reads pixels from selected other layers as a backdrop. The set of source layers is chosen via a separate dialog; Canvas mode is typically required for shaders that sample their own input.

properties.label: Fade In Time
properties.description: Fade-in duration in seconds. Converted to a frame count at render time using the sequence frame interval. When > 0, the In_Transition_Type / Adjust / Reverse settings become active. 0 means no fade-in.

properties.label: In Transition Type
properties.description: Visual transition style applied during the fade-in period.

properties.label: In Transition Adjustment
properties.description: Generic tuning parameter passed to the selected in-transition (0-100). Meaning depends on the transition — e.g. blind count, wipe direction offset, dissolve grain size. Ignored by transitions in transitionsWithoutAdjust (see notes).

properties.label: Reverse
properties.description: Reverses the direction / polarity of the in-transition. Ignored by transitions in transitionsWithoutReverse (see notes) because they are inherently non-directional.

properties.label: Fade Out Time
properties.description: Fade-out duration in seconds. Converted to a frame count at render time. When > 0, the Out_Transition_Type / Adjust / Reverse settings become active. 0 means no fade-out.

properties.label: Out Transition Type
properties.description: Visual transition style applied during the fade-out period.

properties.label: Out Transition Adjustment
properties.description: Generic tuning parameter for the selected out-transition (0-100). Same semantics as In_Transition_Adjust — meaning depends on the transition type. Ignored by transitions in transitionsWithoutAdjust.

properties.label: Reverse
properties.description: Reverses the direction / polarity of the out-transition. Ignored by transitions in transitionsWithoutReverse.

notes: ResetTimingPanel is UI-only — it's persisted under T_ but the render engine never reads it. Included in this file because it's one of the T_-prefixed keys that appears in sequence XML.

LayerMethod and EffectLayerMix are *also* surfaced in the Buffer panel and serialized as B_CHOICE_LayerMethod / B_SLIDER_EffectLayerMix. When both T_ and B_ copies exist on the same effect, the T_ copy is the authoritative value — B_ is a legacy pass-through kept for backward compatibility.

In_Transition_Adjust / Out_Transition_Adjust and their Reverse checkboxes are only meaningful when the corresponding Fade time is > 0 AND the transition type supports that parameter. See transitionsWithoutAdjust and transitionsWithoutReverse for the exclusion lists (derived from src-ui-wx/ui/sequencer/TimingPanel.cpp::ValidateWindow).

Canvas mode requires a separate layer-selection dialog invoked from the panel; the selected source layers are persisted in a different setting key (T_BUTTON_Layers or similar) which is NOT documented here because it is a UI-dialog payload, not a render input. PixelBuffer reads canvas layer data via a different code path.

Suppress/Freeze frame settings are read by PixelBuffer::SetLayerSettings and gate the per-frame effect rendering — they don't affect the effect's output frames themselves, just which of those frames are actually composited.
Fade in/out times are specified in seconds in the UI but stored as float strings and converted to integer step counts at render time using the sequence's frameTimeInMs.
